prompt: Leadership wants SAM entitlements, HAM inventory, and Finance depreciation aligned on one laptop record. Which integration strategy should the architect recommend?
choices: Synchronize asset, CI, and financial attributes with shared model categories, IRE rules, and governed lifecycle workflows across ITAM applications
Maintain separate spreadsheets for each team without CMDB or asset synchronization
Disable asset-to-CI creation so SAM and HAM never share identifiers
Import finance data only after all hardware assets are retired from the instance
explanation: Mature ITAM programs integrate SAM, HAM, and financial records through shared models, CMDB governance, and lifecycle workflows.
domain: Operational Integration of IT Asset Management Processes

prompt: A legacy asset database must migrate into ServiceNow before HAM go-live. Which approach reduces cutover risk?
choices: Phased import with field normalization, pilot validation in sub-production, and reconciliation against Discovery before production cutover
Bulk load all records into production without validation to meet the deadline
Skip serial numbers during migration and rely on users to update assets after go-live
Retire every existing asset in ServiceNow so migration starts from empty tables
explanation: Data migration should use governed imports, normalization, and reconciliation pilots before trusting legacy data in production HAM processes.
domain: Operational Integration of IT Asset Management Processes

prompt: CMDB CIs and HAM assets frequently drift out of sync after Discovery runs. What reconciliation design should the team implement?
choices: Use model categories, IRE identification rules, and asset-CI sync properties with regular CMDB Success Advisor monitoring
Disable Discovery for all hardware classes to prevent CI updates
Delete CIs whenever asset assignment changes without updating related records
Create assets manually for every CI without synchronization or identification keys
explanation: Asset-CI alignment depends on model category governance, IRE rules, sync configuration, and ongoing data quality monitoring.
domain: Operational Integration of IT Asset Management Processes

prompt: Discovery will scan 40,000 endpoints across datacenters and branch offices during HAM rollout. How should scope and phasing be planned?
choices: Pilot high-value locations first, validate reconciliation results, then expand schedules by region with change-controlled credential and scope updates
Enable full RFC1918 scanning globally on day one without credential testing
Limit Discovery to stockrooms only and skip in-use deployed devices
Run Discovery only after every asset is manually assigned to avoid duplicates
explanation: Discovery rollout should phase by location, validate reconciliation outcomes, and expand scope with governed change control.
domain: Operational Integration of IT Asset Management Processes

prompt: Before HAM go-live, which readiness checklist items should the program office validate with stakeholders?
choices: Stockrooms configured, models normalized, roles assigned, pilot fulfillment completed, and training delivered for asset, stockroom, and requester personas
Every business service map finalized enterprise-wide before any hardware model is created
All software entitlements reconciled globally before receiving the first laptop
Discovery schedules disabled until two years of operational data is collected
explanation: Go-live readiness requires validated foundation data, process pilots, role-based training, and stakeholder sign-off on core HAM workflows.
domain: Operational Integration of IT Asset Management Processes
